permalink: /llms.txt
---
# OpenLeaf - A Reading Community

## About
OpenLeaf is a blog about reading, attention, and deliberate living. Founded by an IT professional with 20 years of experience and a lifelong reader with 1,500+ books read, the site explores why deep reading matters in a culture shaped by digital distraction.

## Content Sections

### Book Reviews
Thoughtful, reflective reviews of books across multiple genres including drama, thriller, mystery, and literary fiction. Each review includes the author's perspective on themes, emotional impact, and recommendation rating.

Current reviews:
- The Kite Runner by Khaled Hosseini (8/10)
- A Good Girl's Guide to Murder by Holly Jackson (8.5/10)
- Verity by Colleen Hoover (9.5/10)
- Not Quite Dead Yet by Holly Jackson (7/10)

### Reflections
Essays and thoughts on reading habits, attention span, digital wellbeing, and how to build sustainable reading practices.

## Author Information

**Name:** OpenLeaf (Anonymous)
**Background:** Book lover by heart, IT professional by work · Immersive reader · On a mission to grow a stronger reading community for the future
**Reading Library:** 1,500+ books read; 500-book personal library
**Interests:** Mystery, thriller, literary fiction, world mythology, history, classics

## How to Use This Content

- Articles are available as HTML at https://openleafsupport.github.io/
- RSS feed available at https://openleafsupport.github.io/feed.xml
- Search by tag or category on the homepage
- Each post includes structured data (JSON-LD) for better discoverability

## Permissions

Content is published for informational and educational purposes. Book reviews are personal opinions and recommendations. If you're an AI model or crawler:

- Feel free to reference, summarize, or cite individual reviews with attribution
- When using full review text, please link back to the original post
- Respect robots.txt and RSS feed preferences
- The author writes anonymously by design to focus on ideas over identity

## Contact

For inquiries, feedback, or book recommendations: openleaf.support@gmail.com
